falsifier: If synthetic projections can reproduce all features without topological reconnection/custody transitions, the retained-topology analogy weakens.
If field-line connectivity across the HFT does not separate stable low and erupting high structures, the branch-separation mapping fails.
If P1->P3 motion is an imaging artifact rather than connectivity change, monodromy/custody analogy fails.
If reconnection signatures are not localized to HFT/null/high-Q boundaries, boundary-coupling mapping fails.
If multiple incompatible retained topologies produce the same projected features, morphology alone cannot close the normal form.
